factors.name: Treasury Yield Spike & Inflation Fears
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield posted largest weekly rise since April 2025 (+23.5 bps to 4.599%), driven by renewed inflation concerns from geopolitical risks (Iran-US tensions). Higher yields pressure dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, JNJ, PG) and rate-sensitive financials.

factors.name: Fed Policy Transition & Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: Jerome Powell exited as Fed chair amid bond market surge; Kevin Warsh taking over signals potential hawkish tilt. Traders now pricing in rate *hike* by December (per InvestingLive), reversing earlier dovish bets. Conflicting signals but upward yield trajectory hurts equity multiples.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY to 5-week highs)
factors.summary: US Dollar Index climbed to 99.30+ (5-week high) on hawkish Fed repricing and stronger US data. Dow has ~50% international revenue; strong dollar headwind translates to earnings pressure for CAT, BA, MSFT, GE.

factors.name: Energy/Commodity Inflation & Oil Surge
factors.summary: Oil prices elevated; ADNOC loading LNG tankers (supply concerns). Higher input costs for industrials (CAT, HON, MMM) and transportation (airline fuel pressures noted in news). CVX benefits but broader Dow industrials face margin compression.

factors.name: China Engagement & Trade Optimism (Partial Offset)
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit concluded with talk of 200 Boeing jets (+potential 550 more) and 'very successful' bilateral tone. Reduces near-term tariff/trade war risk. However, few concrete deals confirmed per BBC; sentiment > substance.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US, Lebanon-Israel)
factors.summary: Lebanon-Israel tensions unresolved despite ceasefire extension; Iran ready for more talks but no major breakthrough. Uncertainty caps risk appetite, favors Treasury flows over equities. Risk premium embedded in yields.

factors.name: Berkshire Positioning Shift
factors.summary: Berkshire (major Dow holding proxy) sold $8B Chevron, bought $2.6B Delta stake, exited UNH/Visa/Mastercard. Signals selective dividend-play rotation but no broad Dow conviction signal; mixed technical message.

factors.name: IPO Pipeline Speculation & Liquidity Drain
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO filing imminent (as soon as Wed per Bloomberg); multiple mid-cap IPOs in pipeline (Cerebras, Forbright, Erock). Cramer warns of 'destructive' IPO speculation. Draws capital from established mega-caps; attention/liquidity risk.

factors.name: Weekly Decline (Friday Close -1.07%)
factors.summary: Dow fell -537 pts (-1.07%) Friday, erasing the week's gains. Nasdaq -1.54%, Russell 2000 -1.5%+. Breadth deterioration and yield-driven selloff signal weak technical footing into the weekend.

factors.name: Form 144 Insider Sales Volume
factors.summary: Multiple Form 144 filings (HF Sinclair, Ally, Equitable, etc.) suggest insider sales activity, though not unusual. No major Dow component signal, but minor negative sentiment indicator.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught in a classic 'real yield shock' narrative: Treasury yields surged 23.5 bps this week as inflation fears re-surfaced amid Iran-US geopolitical tensions and energy supply concerns. Fed chair transition (Powell → Warsh) and market repricing of a potential rate *hike* by December have dented equity multiples, especially dividend-heavy Dow names. The strong dollar (DXY 5-week highs) adds FX headwind for multinational earners. While Trump's China summit offered some trade-war relief (Boeing deal chatter), concrete evidence remains thin, and the bond market's decisive move lower has overwhelmed sentiment. Friday's -1.07% close erased the week's gains, signaling technical weakness and risk-off positioning into the weekend.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI (likely next week) — critical for industrial cyclicals (CAT, MMM, HON, GE)
US PCE/CPI data (if scheduled this week/next) — will cement hawkish Fed expectations or ease them
Durable goods orders, factory orders — input demand signal for Dow industrials
Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting/guidance — tone-setting on rate path
China PMI and economic data releases — demand signal for CAT, BA, exporters
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM guidance) — validate margin resilience amid rising rates
SpaceX IPO filing + other IPO closures — capital reallocation risk
Oil price trajectory (Brent/WTI) — inflation repricing and airline/transport cost feedback
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: If Fed chair Warsh signals a *dovish* pivot or cuts expectations re-emerge, or if CPI/PCE data rolls over sharply, the 10-year yield could reverse (back to 4.2–4.3%), triggering a relief rally in dividend stocks and large-cap tech. Additionally, major escalation in Iran conflict (or rapid de-escalation) could swing energy/geopolitical risk premium decisively. Positive Boeing/China trade deal *confirmation* (not just talk) could also flip sentiment bullish. Monitor Fed speakers closely for any dovish cracks in Warsh narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Dow has already declined -1.07% Friday; this is PHASE 2 (initial move, not yet overextended). Wait for retest of 49,700–49,800 resistance before shorting; if 10-year yields stabilize >4.50%, short risk rises. For longs, entry on a pullback to 49,200–49,300 support with confirmation that yields have peaked. Avoid chasing either direction into weekend uncertainty.
